commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: External PR still needs redacted real ACP behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live-environment proof and no narrow code repair finding is queued.; Cleared: The diff is limited to ACP cleanup logic and tests, with no dependency, workflow, permission, secret-handling, network, or package execution changes. (sha=66007fdd20d518144df4f63b925b8b5978e5517f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared ACP stale-session helper for missing runtime cwd errors, applies it to dispatch and resetAcpSessionInPlace cleanup, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main omits the missing working-directory phrase from dispatch stale-session cleanup and resetAcpSessionInPlace ignores closeSession runtimeNotice results. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: External PR still needs redacted real ACP behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's live-environment proof and no narrow code repair finding is queued.

target.title: fix(acp): treat missing cwd as stale bound session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: bound ACP sessions stayed attached after the runtime `cwd` disappeared, so retries kept failing against a dead session.
- Why it matters: affected threads remain wedged until someone manually clears the stale binding.
- What changed: treat missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors as stale-session signals in both dispatch-time cleanup and reset-in-place cleanup, and cover both paths with regressions.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stale cleanup still only triggers for `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; this does not broaden cleanup to generic turn failures.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #60720
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: stale-session detection only recognized metadata and resource-not-found init failures, so a missing ACP runtime working directory did not trigger stale unbind.
- Missing detection / guardrail: reset-in-place failure handling returned the error without clearing the stale binding, and no regression covered missing-`cwd` init failures.
- Contributing context (if known): the old fix branch had drifted too far from current `main`, but the bug still reproduced on April 18, 2026.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`, `src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: missing-`cwd` `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` errors unbind stale bindings during both dispatch and reset-in-place recovery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: both failure paths are local runtime/control-plane seams and do not need a live ACP backend to prove the cleanup behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Bound ACP conversations recover automatically when the runtime working directory is gone instead of staying wedged behind a stale binding.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [binding remains] -> [next attempt fails again]

After:
[bound ACP turn/reset] -> [missing cwd init failure] -> [stale binding removed] -> [next attempt can recreate]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: NixOS Linux
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22.21.1, pnpm 11.1.0, OpenClaw package 2026.5.12-beta.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP persistent bindings
- Relevant config (redacted): session-bound ACP conversation with runtime `cwd` configured

### Steps

1. Reproduce an `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` error whose message is `ACP runtime working directory does not exist: ...` on a bound ACP session.
2. Trigger either dispatch-time reply handling or `resetAcpSessionInPlace` for that session.
3. Observe whether the session binding is removed.

### Expected

- The stale binding is removed and the failure is returned as skipped/recoverable cleanup, so the next attempt can recreate state.

### Actual

- Before this change, the stale binding remained attached and the same failure repeated.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reproduced the missing-`cwd` classification gap against current `main`; ran targeted ACP dispatch/reset tests after the fix.
- Edge cases checked: stale-binding cleanup failure still returns the original skipped error instead of masking it.
- What you did **not** verify: live ACP backend behavior outside the focused test harness.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: stale-session cleanup could be triggered too broadly.
  - Mitigation: the shared helper still requires `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, and the new coverage locks the intended missing-`cwd` path specifically.

## AI Assistance

- AI was used to reproduce the current bug on `main`, implement the fix, run targeted tests, and draft this PR body.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A bound ACP session whose persisted runtime `cwd` no longer exists should be treated as stale, unbind the affected conversation, and return recoverable/skipped cleanup instead of keeping the stale binding attached.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux; Node.js v22.21.1; pnpm 11.1.0; OpenClaw package version 2026.5.12-beta.1; base `origin/main` at `9eed27a9b8`; branch `fix/acp-stale-cwd-replacement` at `66007fdd20`. This used the actual OpenClaw repo test runner and source tree.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  41 passed (41)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 13.42s

$ pnpm test src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts -t resetAcpSessionInPlace
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed | 4 skipped (11)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 4.10s

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.ts src/acp/persistent-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 25ms on 5 files using 1 threads.

$ timeout -k5s 300s codex review --base origin/main
The change consistently extends stale ACP session detection to missing working directories and unbinds affected session bindings in both dispatch and configured-binding reset paths. I did not find a discrete correctness issue introduced by the diff.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The missing-cwd dispatch path calls `unbind({ targetSessionKey, reason: "acp-session-init-failed" })`; the reset path handles both thrown missing-cwd errors and returned `runtimeNotice` missing-cwd errors by unbinding stale bindings and returning `{ ok: false, skipped: true, error: ... }`.
- **What was not tested**: Full live ACP backend recovery outside this local OpenClaw checkout was not exercised; the proof is focused on the implicated OpenClaw dispatch/reset seams.
target.author: kesslerio

commands.reason: ClawSweeper automation was paused by a later /clawsweeper stop command

commands.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix: directly deliver completed media fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep generated media tasks active until completion delivery is confirmed, so duplicate/retry checks see the task while delivery is still pending.
- Add duplicate and recent-completion guards for image, video, and music generation requests.
- Add a direct generated-media fallback when requester-agent/message-tool delivery misses the completed media, while preserving the active wake/message-tool handoff path from `upstream/main`.
- Address review follow-up by preserving concurrent distinct image prompts, keeping same-prompt duplicate guards, reporting all active image tasks, and updating the generated-media fallback docs.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/video-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree HEAD upstream/main`

## Review follow-up verification

Current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` addresses the ClawSweeper findings from the durable review comment:

- Same-prompt image retries still return active/recent status, while distinct image prompts can start their own task.
- Recent duplicate guards now keep multiple recent media starts per session/tool so a completed prompt remains guarded after another prompt starts.
- `image_generate` `action:"status"` reports every active image task instead of hiding concurrent image work behind a single task.
- `docs/automation/tasks.md` and `docs/tools/media-overview.md` document the direct generated-media fallback.

Current-head commands after those updates:

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts` -> 2 files, 46 tests passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts` -> 2 files, 57 tests passed.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` -> passed.
- `pnpm format -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts` -> passed after the lint fix.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main` -> lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `docs`.
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main` -> passed.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated media completions keep the task/session delivery path active until completion delivery is confirmed. If active requester wake and requester-agent/message-tool delivery miss the generated media, the PR fallback posts one direct channel media message to the original chat. The follow-up commit also prevents duplicate image-generation task handling from collapsing distinct image prompts while preserving same-prompt duplicate guards.

Real environment tested: Live Discord transport proof was collected from this PR branch at `fe92ef21ce51888cba717edd999a4ef90772a28b` (`pnpm openclaw --help` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (fe92ef2)`) against live Discord channel transport. Target channel is redacted as `discord channel:...5762`; the live fallback message id was `1506455834782072842`. After the review-follow-up updates, current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` was verified with the current-head tests/checks above. The follow-up commit did not change the direct Discord fallback transport boundary exercised by the live proof; it changed image duplicate-guard/status behavior and docs.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Live fallback proof used an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script from the PR checkout. The script resolved command-scoped Discord secrets for the configured channel, loaded the configured Discord plugin, called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement(...)` from `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`, forced active requester wake to return `queued:false` with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, forced the requester-agent handoff to return no message-tool/media delivery evidence, and let the PR fallback call the real `sendMessage(...)` transport. Then I fetched the exact Discord message by id with `handleDiscordAction({ action: "fetchMessage", guildId: "<redacted>", channelId: "...5762", messageId: "1506455834782072842" })` and checked the active session mirror line with `sed -n '19p' <state>/agents/main/sessions/6c7c13d6-d515-4cf4-ad90-ba2a84ef203f.jsonl | jq ...`. After the current-head follow-up patch, I reran the focused task/status/fallback tests, formatting checks, `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`, and autoreview listed in Review follow-up verification.

Evidence after fix: Redacted console output from the live proof run:

```text
active requester wake:
  waitForTranscriptCommit: true
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  steeringMode: all

requester-agent handoff:
  method: agent
  expectFinal: true
  deliver: false
  channel: discord
  to: channel:...5762
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  idempotencyKey: PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145
  mediaEvents: 1

direct fallback result:
  delivered: true
  path: direct
  phase: direct-primary
  phaseDelivered: true

sendMessage receipt:
  messageId: 1506455834782072842
  platformMessageIds: [1506455834782072842]
  parts: [{ kind: media, index: 0 }]
```

Exact live Discord readback after the fallback send:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "message": {
    "id": "1506455834782072842",
    "content": "The generated image is ready.",
    "timestampUtc": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.608Z",
    "authorBot": true,
    "attachmentCount": 1,
    "attachment": {
      "filename": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
      "size": 91,
      "width": 1,
      "height": 1,
      "content_type": "image/png"
    }
  }
}
```

Transcript mirror proof from the active Discord session after delivery:

```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.973Z",
  "role": "assistant",
  "text": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
  "provider": "openclaw",
  "model": "delivery-mirror",
  "stopReason": "stop",
  "idempotencyKey": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-177923...generated-media-direct"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The fallback produced exactly one Discord bot message in the original channel with the fixed completion text and exactly one `image/png` attachment. The active wake/requester-agent message-tool path was attempted first and missed media, then the direct fallback delivered once and the active session mirror committed after the send. Current-head tests additionally prove same-prompt image duplicate calls are guarded, distinct image prompts start separate tasks, recent success guards survive concurrent image prompts, and status reports all active image tasks.

What was not tested: Mantis Telegram Desktop visual proof did not run because workflow `26133709129` skipped downstream proof jobs after reporting actor `fuller-stack-dev` had only read permission. This proof uses live Discord transport, live Discord readback, and the real session mirror path instead. The media generator provider itself was not called; the proof injected the completed generated-media event at the delivery boundary while the fallback transport/readback/transcript surfaces were real. Live Discord transport was not rerun after the review-follow-up commit because that commit did not change the direct fallback transport path; current-head verification covered the changed duplicate-guard/status/docs behavior.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix: directly deliver completed media fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep generated media tasks active until completion delivery is confirmed, so duplicate/retry checks see the task while delivery is still pending.
- Add duplicate and recent-completion guards for image, video, and music generation requests.
- Add a direct generated-media fallback when requester-agent/message-tool delivery misses the completed media, while preserving the active wake/message-tool handoff path from `upstream/main`.
- Address review follow-up by preserving concurrent distinct image prompts, keeping same-prompt duplicate guards, reporting all active image tasks, and updating the generated-media fallback docs.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/video-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree HEAD upstream/main`

## Review follow-up verification

Current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` addresses the ClawSweeper findings from the durable review comment:

- Same-prompt image retries still return active/recent status, while distinct image prompts can start their own task.
- Recent duplicate guards now keep multiple recent media starts per session/tool so a completed prompt remains guarded after another prompt starts.
- `image_generate` `action:"status"` reports every active image task instead of hiding concurrent image work behind a single task.
- `docs/automation/tasks.md` and `docs/tools/media-overview.md` document the direct generated-media fallback.

Current-head commands after those updates:

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts` -> 2 files, 46 tests passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts` -> 2 files, 57 tests passed.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` -> passed.
- `pnpm format -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts` -> passed after the lint fix.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main` -> lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `docs`.
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main` -> passed.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated media completions keep the task/session delivery path active until completion delivery is confirmed. If active requester wake and requester-agent/message-tool delivery miss the generated media, the PR fallback posts one direct channel media message to the original chat. The follow-up commit also prevents duplicate image-generation task handling from collapsing distinct image prompts while preserving same-prompt duplicate guards.

Real environment tested: Live Discord transport proof was collected from this PR branch at `fe92ef21ce51888cba717edd999a4ef90772a28b` (`pnpm openclaw --help` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (fe92ef2)`) against live Discord channel transport. Target channel is redacted as `discord channel:...5762`; the live fallback message id was `1506455834782072842`. After the review-follow-up updates, current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` was verified with the current-head tests/checks above. The follow-up commit did not change the direct Discord fallback transport boundary exercised by the live proof; it changed image duplicate-guard/status behavior and docs.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Live fallback proof used an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script from the PR checkout. The script resolved command-scoped Discord secrets for the configured channel, loaded the configured Discord plugin, called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement(...)` from `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`, forced active requester wake to return `queued:false` with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, forced the requester-agent handoff to return no message-tool/media delivery evidence, and let the PR fallback call the real `sendMessage(...)` transport. Then I fetched the exact Discord message by id with `handleDiscordAction({ action: "fetchMessage", guildId: "<redacted>", channelId: "...5762", messageId: "1506455834782072842" })` and checked the active session mirror line with `sed -n '19p' <state>/agents/main/sessions/6c7c13d6-d515-4cf4-ad90-ba2a84ef203f.jsonl | jq ...`. After the current-head follow-up patch, I reran the focused task/status/fallback tests, formatting checks, `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`, and autoreview listed in Review follow-up verification.

Evidence after fix: Redacted console output from the live proof run:

```text
active requester wake:
  waitForTranscriptCommit: true
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  steeringMode: all

requester-agent handoff:
  method: agent
  expectFinal: true
  deliver: false
  channel: discord
  to: channel:...5762
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  idempotencyKey: PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145
  mediaEvents: 1

direct fallback result:
  delivered: true
  path: direct
  phase: direct-primary
  phaseDelivered: true

sendMessage receipt:
  messageId: 1506455834782072842
  platformMessageIds: [1506455834782072842]
  parts: [{ kind: media, index: 0 }]
```

Exact live Discord readback after the fallback send:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "message": {
    "id": "1506455834782072842",
    "content": "The generated image is ready.",
    "timestampUtc": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.608Z",
    "authorBot": true,
    "attachmentCount": 1,
    "attachment": {
      "filename": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
      "size": 91,
      "width": 1,
      "height": 1,
      "content_type": "image/png"
    }
  }
}
```

Transcript mirror proof from the active Discord session after delivery:

```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.973Z",
  "role": "assistant",
  "text": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
  "provider": "openclaw",
  "model": "delivery-mirror",
  "stopReason": "stop",
  "idempotencyKey": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-177923...generated-media-direct"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The fallback produced exactly one Discord bot message in the original channel with the fixed completion text and exactly one `image/png` attachment. The active wake/requester-agent message-tool path was attempted first and missed media, then the direct fallback delivered once and the active session mirror committed after the send. Current-head tests additionally prove same-prompt image duplicate calls are guarded, distinct image prompts start separate tasks, recent success guards survive concurrent image prompts, and status reports all active image tasks.

What was not tested: Mantis Telegram Desktop visual proof did not run because workflow `26133709129` skipped downstream proof jobs after reporting actor `fuller-stack-dev` had only read permission. This proof uses live Discord transport, live Discord readback, and the real session mirror path instead. The media generator provider itself was not called; the proof injected the completed generated-media event at the delivery boundary while the fallback transport/readback/transcript surfaces were real. Live Discord transport was not rerun after the review-follow-up commit because that commit did not change the direct fallback transport path; current-head verification covered the changed duplicate-guard/status/docs behavior.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix: directly deliver completed media fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep generated media tasks active until completion delivery is confirmed, so duplicate/retry checks see the task while delivery is still pending.
- Add duplicate and recent-completion guards for image, video, and music generation requests.
- Add a direct generated-media fallback when requester-agent/message-tool delivery misses the completed media, while preserving the active wake/message-tool handoff path from `upstream/main`.
- Address review follow-up by preserving concurrent distinct image prompts, keeping same-prompt duplicate guards, reporting all active image tasks, and updating the generated-media fallback docs.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/video-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree HEAD upstream/main`

Current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` addresses the ClawSweeper findings from the durable review comment:
